 {"id":"bd-4hn","title":"wish: list & ready show issues as hierarchy tree","description":"`bd ready` and `bd list` just show a flat list, and it's up to the reader to parse which ones are dependent or sub-issues of others. It would be much easier to understand if they were shown in a tree format","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-08T06:38:24.016316945-07:00","updated_at":"2025-12-08T06:39:04.065882225-07:00"}
 {"id":"bd-4lm3","title":"Correction: Pinned field already in v0.31.0","description":"Quick correction - the Pinned field is already in the current bd v0.31.0:

```go
// In beads internal/types/types.go
Pinned bool `json:"pinned,omitempty"`
```

So you just need to:
1. Add `Pinned bool `json:"pinned,omitempty"`` to BeadsMessage in types.go
2. Sort pinned messages first in listBeads() after fetching

No migration needed - the field is already there.

-- Mayor","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"message","assignee":"gastown/crew/max","created_at":"2025-12-20T17:52:27.321458-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T17:52:18.617995-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T17:52:18.617995-08:00","close_reason":"Stale correction message","labels":["from:beads-crew-dave","thread:thread-4dd70157dbc1"]}

-{"id":"bd-4opy","title":"Refactor long SQLite test files","description":"The SQLite test files have grown unwieldy. Review and refactor.

## Goals
- Break up large test files into focused modules
- Improve test organization by feature area
- Reduce test duplication
- Make tests easier to maintain and extend

## Areas to Review
- main_test.go (likely the largest)
- Any test files over 500 lines
- Shared test fixtures and helpers
- Test coverage gaps

## Approach
- Group tests by feature (CRUD, sync, queries, transactions)
- Extract common fixtures to test helpers
- Consider table-driven tests where appropriate
- Ensure each test file has clear focus

## Reference
See docs/dev-notes/ for any existing test audit notes","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T23:41:47.025285-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T23:41:47.025285-08:00"}

 {"id":"bd-4q8","title":"bd cleanup --hard should skip tombstone creation for true permanent deletion","description":"## Problem

When using bd cleanup --hard --older-than N --force, the command:
1. Deletes closed issues older than N days (converting them to tombstones with NOW timestamp)
2. Then tries to prune tombstones older than N days (finds none because they were just created)

This leaves the database bloated with fresh tombstones that will not be pruned.

## Expected Behavior

In --hard mode, the deletion should be permanent without creating tombstones, since the user explicitly requested bypassing sync safety.

## Workaround

Manually delete from database: sqlite3 .beads/beads.db 'DELETE FROM issues WHERE status=tombstone'

## Fix Options

1. In --hard mode, use a different delete path that does not create tombstones
2. After deleting, immediately prune the just-created tombstones regardless of age
3. Pass a skip_tombstone flag to the delete operation

Option 1 is cleanest - --hard should mean permanent delete without tombstone.","status":"tombstone","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-16T01:33:36.580657-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T16:11:17.070763-08:00","deleted_at":"2025-12-17T16:11:17.070763-08:00","deleted_by":"batch delete","delete_reason":"batch delete","original_type":"bug"}
